operating_state: Net revenues increased 15% to $14.58 billion driven by Global Banking & Markets growth from higher Advisory revenues and 36% Equities expansion in intermediation and financing activities, plus FICC growth from mortgage and structured lending. Asset & Wealth Management revenues declined from significantly lower investment returns partially offset by higher management fees from increased assets under supervision. Platform Solutions remained flat as transaction banking declined from lower deposit balances.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased 8% to $9.24 billion from higher compensation reflecting improved performance and increased transaction-based expenses, improving efficiency ratio to 63.4% from 67.0% prior year. Credit provisions increased to $384 million driven by credit card portfolio charge-offs and wholesale portfolio growth.
material_events: The firm returned $3.96 billion to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends, with first-half capital returns reaching $9.29 billion. The Board approved a $40 billion share repurchase program with no expiration and increased quarterly dividends from $3.00 to $4.00 per share beginning Q3 2025. The firm completed debt issuances and shareholders approved the 2025 Stock Incentive Plan extending through 2029.
macro_exposures: Operations faced continued market volatility and macroeconomic uncertainty including inflation concerns, trade policy changes, geopolitical tensions, and central bank policy uncertainty. The FRB announced a 3.4% stress capital buffer requirement effective October 2025 with proposed rules potentially resulting in 4.9% stress capital buffer beginning January 2026. Credit exposure increased from growth in loans and lending commitments, with non-investment-grade counterparty exposures rising primarily from reduced central bank deposits.
forward_outlook: Management expects 2025 annual effective tax rate of approximately 22% and maintains through-the-cycle ROE targets of 14-16% with ROTE targets of 15-17%. Platform Solutions maintains pre-tax breakeven target by end of 2025 while Asset & Wealth Management targets mid-teens ROE with mid-twenties pre-tax margins. The firm targets growing total credit alternative assets to $300 billion by end of 2028 and expects to sell down majority of remaining historical principal investments by end of 2026.
uncertainty_profile: Under adverse economic scenario modeling global recession, allowance for credit losses would increase by approximately $0.8 billion. Future performance remains contingent on market conditions with potential negative impacts on market-making activity levels, equity underwriting volumes, and completed M&A transactions. Operational risk exposure includes heightened cybersecurity threats from AI-enabled fraud and ransomware attacks.
